_comment_overview: GatedMILHierarchicalExtractor uses gated MIL (Multiple Instance Learning) attention with K learnable confounder queries. It splits text into sentences, encodes each with tiny BERT, then applies gated attention (tanh * sigmoid gating) to extract K confounder representations. Each task (propensity, tau, outcome) learns to weight these confounders differently. This is causally coherent: confounders affect both treatment and outcome, but tasks may weight them differently.

architecture._comment_sentence_encoder: Sentence encoder model from HuggingFace. prajjwal1/bert-tiny is very fast (4.4M params). For better quality, try distilbert-base-uncased or prajjwal1/bert-small.
architecture._comment_freeze: Whether to freeze the sentence encoder weights. True is faster and uses less memory; False allows fine-tuning.
architecture._comment_max_sentences: Maximum sentences per document. Documents with more sentences will have later sentences truncated.
architecture._comment_max_sentence_length: Maximum tokens per sentence for BERT encoding. Sentences longer than this are truncated.
architecture._comment_hidden_dim: Hidden dimension for gated MIL attention layers (V, U, W projections).
architecture._comment_num_confounders: Number of confounder queries (K). Each query learns to attend to different confounding information in the text. More confounders = more expressiveness but may overfit on small datasets.
architecture._comment_dropout: Dropout rate in attention and projection layers.
architecture._comment_projection_dim: Final output dimension of the feature extractor. This feeds into DragonNet/RLearner.
architecture._comment_hierarchical: Token-level hierarchical mode. When true, applies token-level gated attention to create K confounder-specific sentence representations. Preserves fine-grained distinctions like 'ECOG PS 0' vs 'ECOG PS 2'.
architecture._comment_token_hidden_dim: Hidden dimension for token-level gated attention. Only used when gated_mil_hierarchical=true.
